data-refinement.title: Data refinement & hygiene — fix NULL descriptions, notes, types, edges, and chunk gaps across the entire graph
data-refinement.notes:
Critical prerequisite for bot quality. The KB has 4K nodes but most have NULL descriptions, NULL notes, wrong node_types, and junk edges. Bots can only be as good as the data they retrieve.

Depends on: content-ingestion (DONE), schema-and-port (DONE)
Blocks: discord-bot-v2 (bot quality depends on clean data)

tasks.text: Phase 0: Audit — sample nodes by type, check edges, chunk coverage, duplicates
Phase 0.5: Evaluate graph structure — hub nodes vs flat types vs hybrid (see PRD open question)
Phase 0.5: Decide and implement graph structure (if hubs: create nodes + edges)
Phase 1: Build scripts/refine-data.ts with --audit, --dry-run, --task, --limit
Task: Fix node_types — migrate episode→podcast/meetup/paper-club, source→article/newsletter
Task: Generate descriptions for all 1,736 NULL-description nodes
Task: Generate notes for all 1,670 NULL-notes nodes
Task: Clean edges — rewrite 2,619 ai_similarity JSON blobs to human-readable, fix 100 NULL contexts
Task: Fill chunk gaps — chunk+embed 1,532 nodes missing from chunks table
Verify: Re-run --audit, confirm zero NULLs, spot-check quality

discord-bot-v2.title: Discord bot refinement — contextual grounding, content-type awareness, temporal intelligence, channel architecture
discord-bot-v2.notes:
Bots are live and working but need deeper grounding. They need to understand WHAT Latent Space is, navigate content by type and time, and operate in a proper channel architecture (bot-talk channel + user thread model).

Depends on: data-refinement (clean data = better retrieval), discord-bot v1 (DONE)
Blocks: LS Discord rollout

tasks.text: Rewrite start-here guide with full LS identity, content types, and temporal context
Write content-types guide covering podcast/article/newsletter/meetup/paper-club with query patterns
Add temporal awareness — bots understand dates, can answer 'recent', 'last month', 'timeline of X'
Update Sig persona — grounded in LS identity, cites with type+date context
Update Slop persona — grounded in LS identity, hot takes reference specific content
Implement bot-talk channel — dedicated channel where Sig & Slop engage each other on new material
Implement user thread model — user question creates thread, ongoing conversation lives in thread
Test end-to-end in test Discord server
Demo to swyx
Add to LS Discord
Stretch: Build daily audio recap pipeline (collate → script → TTS → mix → publish to YouTube)
Stretch: Publish first Sig vs Slop daily recap episode

storylines.title: Storylines / narrative arcs — temporal narrative layer connecting content, entities, and claims over time
storylines.notes:
BBC-inspired narrative layer. A storyline is a node_type='storyline' that tracks arcs like 'the scaling laws debate' or 'open vs closed source AI' across episodes, guests, and claims over time. Enables editorial planning and temporal bot queries.

Future — revisit after core graph is populated and bot is live.

Depends on: schema-and-port (DONE), content-ingestion (DONE), data-refinement

tasks.text: Define storyline metadata schema
Add storyline edge types (advances, central_to, subsumes)
Add temporal edge metadata (valid_from, valid_until)
Create initial storylines from existing content
Storyline timeline UI component
Bot can answer 'trace the arc of X across episodes'
Auto-detect emerging storylines from new content
